mentalModel.keyInsight: This is a classic 'minimum ways' DP where we build solutions bottom-up by considering each coin denomination and asking 'what's the minimum coins needed if I use this coin?'
mentalModel.intuition: For each amount, try every coin and see which one leads to the minimum total coins. The key insight is that dp[amount] = min(dp[amount-coin] + 1) for all valid coins
mentalModel.analogy: Like making change at a store - for any amount, you check which bills/coins you have and pick the combination that uses the fewest total pieces
mentalModel.commonMistake: Trying to use greedy approach (always pick largest coin first) which doesn't work when coin denominations aren't canonical (e.g., coins=[1,3,4], amount=6)

constraintIntuition.criticalConstraint: The constraint that coins can be reused unlimited times makes this an unbounded knapsack variant, not 0/1 knapsack
constraintIntuition.scalingLimit: Solution scales as O(amount * coins.length) which becomes problematic when amount > 10^4 and many coin types
constraintIntuition.alternativeApproach: For very large amounts, could use BFS from target amount down to 0, but DP bottom-up is generally more intuitive
constraintIntuition.constraintPattern: Unlimited use + minimum optimization = unbounded DP pattern

patternEvolution.patternFamily: Dynamic Programming - Unbounded Knapsack/Coin Change variant
patternEvolution.variation: This is the 'minimum ways' variant (vs counting all ways or checking possibility)
patternEvolution.recognitionSignals: Minimum/maximum + unlimited items + building up to target = unbounded DP
patternEvolution.evolutionInsight: Understanding that coin problems are really about exploring all possible ways to build up a value, and DP lets us cache optimal subproblems

problemConnections.uniqueDifference: This seeks minimum count while Coin Change II counts total ways, and Unbounded Knapsack maximizes value
problemConnections.underlyingPrinciple: All are about trying every item multiple times and optimizing some criterion while building up to target

futureApplication.recognitionSignal: Minimum/maximum + can reuse items unlimited times + target sum/value
futureApplication.applicableTo: Minimum steps problems, optimal resource allocation, making change problems, minimum squares/cubes to reach number
futureApplication.teachingMethod: Start with base case (dp[0] = 0), then for each amount ask 'if I use this coin, what's the minimum total?' Take the best option across all coins
